reasoning: The current value of the STLFSI4 index is around -0.62 as of late March 2025. This value represents fairly low financial stress, but is still slightly above its very lowest calm-market "floor" of -0.90 to -1.00 (seen in Jan/Feb 2025 and mid 2024). In the very short term, absent a new financial shock, the index is likely to drift back down towards the -0.80 range, giving a lower probability of an increase by early April (around 41%). However, as the horizon expands, the probability of encountering standard market volatility or a seasonal stock market drop (such as in September, where VIX generally rises and stress increases) begins to outweigh the short-term mean reversion. Over a 1-year horizon, given the highly right-skewed nature of financial stress (a hard floor near -1.0 but potential spikes up to +1 or more during any minor crisis), the probability of the index exceeding its current -0.62 level is slightly above 50% (estimated at 57%). Therefore, probabilities smoothly increase from 41% for a 1-week horizon up to 57% for the 1-year horizon.
